options.content:
**State Transitions per second** tracks your overall cluster throughput (in terms of creating and updating executions). **Total Actions per namespace** tracks "actions" which basically are number of db writes that result from operations like start/signal/update/etc.
**Total RPS** tracks total requests to frontend service on cluster and per namespace.

panels.title: Actions per Namespace
panels.description: Rate of actions broken down by namespace.

panels.title: Total Actions
panels.description: Total rate of actions across all namespaces.

panels.title: RPS per Namespace
panels.description: Rate of frontend service requests broken down by namespace.

panels.title: Total RPS
panels.description: Total rate of requests to the frontend service across all namespaces.

panels.title: Server State Transitions
panels.description: Server state transitions per second

options.content: **Shard Lock latency** is latency acquiring a shard. This latency can often indicate unprovisioned cluster size for workloads running on cluster. **Workflow Lock latency** is latency of persisting updates for single execution, which are done under a workflow lock. High latencies can be related to specific use case, but also high persistence latencies.

panels.title: Shard Lock Latency
panels.description: Latency for acquiring the shard lock (ShardInfo semaphore) on the History service.

panels.title: Workflow Lock Latency
panels.description: Cache latency for HistoryCacheGetOrCreate operation.

options.content: **Persistence Requests, Latencies, and Errors** — this section tracks persistence latencies for your primary Temporal database. Database latencies have a direct effect on cluster performance and end-to-end execution latencies.

panels.title: Persistence Requests per Namespace and Operation
panels.description: Rate of persistence requests for the selected namespace broken down by operation.

panels.title: Persistence Requests Total
panels.description: Total rate of persistence requests across the entire cluster regardless of namespace.

panels.title: Persistence Requests Total per Operation
panels.description: Total rate of persistence requests across the entire cluster broken down by operation.

panels.title: Persistence Latencies
panels.description: Persistence latencies broken down by operation.

panels.title: Persistence Errors by Namespace and Operation
panels.description: Rate of persistence errors for the selected namespace broken down by operation and error type.

panels.title: SQL DB Connection Pool
panels.description: Current SQL DB connection pool stats. Only applicable when using a SQL-based persistence backend (MySQL, PostgreSQL). Not emitted for Cassandra.

panels.title: Persistence Errors Total by Operation
panels.description: Total rate of persistence errors across the entire cluster broken down by operation and error type.

panels.title: Persistence Availability
panels.description: Percentage of persistence requests that succeeded (no errors).

options.content: **Service Latencies** target latencies for different operations, by service type

panels.title: Frontend Service Latency
panels.description: RPC service latency for the Frontend service broken down by operation at the selected percentile.

panels.title: History Service Latency
panels.description: RPC service latency for the History service broken down by operation at the selected percentile.

panels.title: Service Latency No-User Latency
panels.description: Frontend service latency excluding user processing time (no-user latency) broken down by operation at the selected percentile.

panels.title: Service Latency User Latency
panels.description: Frontend service latency attributable to user processing time broken down by operation at the selected percentile.

panels.title: Matching Service Latency
panels.description: RPC service latency for the Matching service broken down by operation at the selected percentile.

options.content: **Service Requests and Errors** — this group tracks service requests and errors for your Temporal cluster frontend services.

panels.title: Service Requests by Namespace
panels.description: Rate of frontend service requests for the selected namespace.

panels.title: Service Requests by Namespace and Operation
panels.description: Rate of frontend service requests broken down across all namespaces and operations.

targets.expr: sum by (namespace, operation) (rate(service_requests{service_name="frontend"}[$__rate_interval]) or on () vector(0))

panels.title: Service Errors by Namespace
panels.description: Rate of service errors on the Frontend service for the selected namespace.

panels.title: Service Errors by Namespace and Operation
panels.description: Rate of frontend service errors broken down across all namespaces and operations.

targets.expr: sum by (namespace, operation) (rate(service_errors{service_name="frontend"}[$__rate_interval]) or on () vector(0))

panels.title: Active gRPC Connections
panels.description: Current number of active gRPC TCP connections per service.

panels.title: gRPC Connection Churn
panels.description: Rate of gRPC TCP connections accepted and closed on the Frontend service.

panels.title: Service Panics
panels.description: Rate of panics across all services. Any value above zero warrants immediate investigation.

panels.title: Server Errors by Type
panels.description: Rate of frontend service errors broken down by error type.

options.content:
**Throttling** tracks APIs that are being throttled. Look for cause `RpsLimit`, `QpsLimit`, `ConcurrentLimit` (limit on max number of pollers), and `SystemOverload` (DB).
**Rate Limits** track current RPS per host and per host and per namespace.

Adjust your dynamic configs if needed.

panels.title: Resource Exhausted with Cause
panels.description: Rate of resource exhausted errors broken down by operation and cause.

panels.title: Actual RPS vs Host RPS Limit
panels.description: Actual frontend request rate per instance overlaid with the configured host RPS limit. When traffic approaches the limit line expect RpsLimit throttle errors.

panels.title: Actual RPS vs Namespace Host RPS Limit
panels.description: Actual frontend request rate per namespace overlaid with the configured namespace host RPS limit.

options.content:
**Busy Workflow Throttling** tracks throttling specific for the `BusyWorkflow` cause. This throttling can be introduced in a number of scenarios, such as very high rate of updates for a single execution (common in fan-out use cases). It can also have a direct correlation to DB latencies.

Note: some dynamic configs such as `EnableWorkflowIdReuseStartTimeValidation` can also introduce throttling on `BusyWorkflow` for operations like `Start`/`SignalWithStart`.

panels.title: Transfer Active Task Errors Discarded
panels.description: Rate of discarded task errors for active transfer operations.

panels.title: Transfer Active Task Errors Limit Exceeded
panels.description: Rate of limit exceeded errors for active transfer operations.

panels.title: Transfer Active Task Errors Workflow Busy
panels.description: Rate of workflow busy errors for active transfer operations.

panels.title: Transfer Active Task Errors Throttled
panels.description: Rate of throttled errors for active transfer operations, broken down by namespace and resource exhausted cause.

options.content:
**Shard Movement** tracks history service shard creation, removal, and closing.

Most commonly this happens during cluster restarts and/or scaling of history hosts. It can also happen due to high DB latencies. Shard movement can affect the cluster intermittently and introduce higher latencies of API operations.

panels.title: Shards Created
panels.description: Rate of shard items created on the History service.

panels.title: Shards Removed
panels.description: Rate of shard items removed on the History service.

panels.title: Shards Closed
panels.description: Rate of shard items closed on the History service.

panels.title: Service Restarts
panels.description: Rate of service restarts broken down by service name.

options.content: **History Timer Task Info** tracks metrics for timer tasks in history service

panels.title: Total Timer Tasks Processed
panels.description: Total rate of timer tasks processed across all active timer operations on the History service.

panels.title: Total Timer Tasks Errors
panels.description: Total rate of errors for timer tasks across all active timer operations on the History service.

panels.title: Timer Task Processing Latency
panels.description: Processing latency for active timer tasks on the History service broken down by operation at the selected percentile.

panels.title: Timer Task Scheduling Latency
panels.description: Scheduling lag for timer tasks on the History service, reflecting how far behind the timer queue is from its scheduled fire time.

panels.title: Workflow Limit Exceeded
panels.description: Rate of workflow tasks failed due to pending activity, child workflow, and cancel request limits being exceeded.

panels.title: Blob Size Errors
panels.description: Rate of requests failed due to blob size limits being exceeded, by namespace.

options.content: **Workflow execution size** tracks metrics for size of workflow executions event history, event count. **Mutable state size** tracks size of workflow execution state (read+write)

panels.title: Workflow History Size
panels.description: p99 size of workflow execution history in bytes by namespace.

panels.title: Workflow History Event Count
panels.description: p99 number of events in workflow execution history by namespace.

panels.title: Mutable State Size
panels.description: p99 size of workflow execution mutable state (emitted on every read and write) by namespace.

panels.title: Persisted Mutable State Size
panels.description: p99 size of workflow execution mutable state written to DB (emitted on writes only) by namespace.

panels.title: Event Blob Size
panels.description: p99 size of individual workflow history event blobs by namespace.

panels.title: Search Attributes Size
panels.description: p99 size of search attributes payload by namespace.

panels.title: Memo Size
panels.description: p99 size of workflow memo payload by namespace.

options.content: **SDK Workers Info** tracks many metrics useful for troubleshooting SDK workers

panels.title: Schedule to Start Latencies
panels.description: Latency from task scheduling to task start, broken down by task type and operation.

panels.title: Tasks Persisted to DB (can indicate task backlog)
panels.description: Rate of CreateTasks persistence requests. A sustained increase may indicate a task backlog building up.

panels.title: Sync Match Latency
panels.description: Sync match latency by operation on the Matching service.

panels.title: Activity StartToClose Timeout
panels.description: Rate of Activity StartToClose timeouts by namespace and operation.

panels.title: Activity ScheduleToStart Timeout
panels.description: Rate of Activity ScheduleToStart timeouts by namespace and operation.

panels.title: Activity Heartbeat Timeout
panels.description: Rate of Activity Heartbeat timeouts by namespace and operation.

panels.title: Workflow Task StartToClose Timeouts per Namespace (sticky tq)
panels.description: Rate of Workflow Task StartToClose timeouts per namespace and operation (sticky task queue).

panels.title: Approximate Task Backlog
panels.description: Approximate backlog count by namespace and task type on the Matching service.

options.content: **Total concurrent pollers** tracks long-poll requests per namespace and per frontend pod

panels.title: Max Concurrent Pollers per Frontend Pod

options.content: **Visibility** tracks your Temporal Visibility store latencies and availability.

panels.title: Visibility Latencies per Operation
panels.description: Visibility task latencies broken down by operation on the History service.

panels.title: Visibility Availability
panels.description: Percentage of visibility requests that succeeded (no errors).

panels.title: Visibility Task End-to-End Latencies
panels.description: End-to-end visibility task latencies (queue latency) broken down by operation on the History service.

panels.title: Visibility Task Processing by Operation
panels.description: Visibility task processing latencies broken down by operation on the History service.

options.content:
**Cluster Replication** tracks metrics related to multi-cluster replication from the **active cluster's perspective** — task generation, send throughput, and stream health.

> **Standby-side monitoring:** Use the dedicated **Temporal Standby Dashboard** for receiver-side lag, DLQ depth, standby task retries, and failover readiness.

> **DLQ panels** marked ⚠️ Cassandra Only require Cassandra persistence and `history.TaskDLQEnabled = true` (default).

panels.title: Replication Task Throughput
panels.description: Rate of replication tasks received, applied, failed, and skipped.

panels.title: Backfill and Duplicate Events
panels.description: Rate of replication backfill tasks processed and duplicate replication events detected.

panels.title: Send and Recv Backlog
panels.description: Average backlog depth on the sender and receiver sides.

panels.title: Tasks Fetched per Batch and Attempts per Task
panels.description: Average number of tasks fetched per batch and average attempts per replication task.

panels.title: Replication Latencies
panels.description: End-to-end, send, queue, processing, and transmission latencies for replication tasks.

panels.title: Backfill Latency
panels.description: Latency of replication backfill task processing.

panels.title: Replication Errors by Type
panels.description: Rate of replication task errors broken down by error type.

panels.title: Stream Health
panels.description: Active cluster stream health signals. replication_stream_stuck means the sender stream to a standby has stalled — any non-zero value warrants immediate investigation.

panels.title: Sender Rate Limit Latency
panels.description: Time the active cluster spent rate-limiting sends to standby clusters. Elevated values here are the active-side cause of standby lag with no other visible signal on the standby. Controlled by history.ReplicationEnableRateLimit dynamic config.

panels.title: Replication Task Generation and Load Latency
panels.description: Generation latency = time from workflow event to replication task creation. Load latency = persistence schedule-to-start for replication tasks. High values here mean the active cluster is slow to produce tasks.

panels.title: Outlier Namespaces
panels.description: Namespaces disproportionately contributing to replication problems. Useful for isolating whether a broad replication issue is caused by a specific namespace.

panels.title: DLQ Writes and Failures ⚠️ Cassandra Only
panels.description: Rate of tasks being written to DLQ and failures to write to DLQ. Only emitted for Cassandra persistence backends (history.TaskDLQEnabled must be true). DLQ enqueue failures are more severe than tasks landing in DLQ — failed tasks cannot even be preserved for inspection. Use tdbg dlq to inspect.

options.content: **Authorization** tracks auth denied requests, auth system failures, and authorization check latency. `service_errors_unauthorized` means auth worked and said no. `service_errors_authorize_failed` means the auth system itself broke — this is the more urgent signal.

panels.title: Unauthorized Requests
panels.description: Rate of requests denied by authorization, broken down by namespace and operation.

panels.title: Authorization System Failures
panels.description: Rate of authorization system failures (auth plugin errors, misconfigurations). Any value above zero warrants immediate investigation.

panels.title: Authorization Check Latency
panels.description: Latency of authorization checks by operation and namespace.
